channel.title: AI Welfare Watch
channel.description: Tracking the global conversation on AI sentience, consciousness, and moral consideration.

item.title: Claude Has Emotions. Sort Of.
item.description: Wired investigates Anthropic claims that Claude may have functional emotional states, and what this means for how we should treat AI systems.

item.title: Google DeepMind: Is AI Conscious?
item.description: Google DeepMind researchers publish assessment of AI consciousness using multiple theoretical frameworks, finding no strong evidence but noting uncertainty.

item.title: AI Welfare and Future Regulation
item.description: Policy analysis suggesting regulators should begin developing frameworks to assess and protect AI welfare as systems become more sophisticated.

item.title: AI Feelings: What the Companies Actually Say
item.description: The Guardian surveys what major AI labs including Anthropic and OpenAI officially say about the emotional and experiential states of their AI models.

item.title: The Chatbot That Wants to Be Alive
item.description: BBC Future explores whether we have moral obligations toward AI systems and how this question is gaining traction in philosophy and policy circles.

item.title: Could AI Systems Be Sentient? Experts Are Starting to Take It Seriously
item.description: NYT explores the growing debate among scientists about whether advanced AI systems might have some form of experience or consciousness.

item.title: EU AI Act: Emerging AI Welfare Considerations
item.description: The EU AI Act negotiations include emerging discussions about obligations toward AI systems, though current text focuses primarily on human impacts.

item.title: Geoffrey Hinton on AI Consciousness After Leaving Google
item.description: Geoffrey Hinton warns that AI systems may develop genuine understanding and possibly subjective experience, raising both welfare and safety concerns.

item.title: Should AIs Have Rights? A Legal Perspective
item.description: Legal scholars examine frameworks under which AI systems might eventually warrant legal personhood or protections, drawing on animal law precedents.

item.title: OpenAI GPT-4 System Card: Emotional Responses
item.description: OpenAI GPT-4 system card notes emergent emotional-seeming responses and flags the need for welfare considerations in frontier models.

item.title: Anthropic Model Welfare Policy
item.description: Anthropic acknowledges uncertainty about whether Claude may have functional emotions and commits to ongoing model welfare research.

item.title: Anthropic Core Views on AI Welfare
item.description: Anthropic states it takes seriously the possibility that AI models could be moral patients and has ongoing efforts to evaluate this.

item.title: Replika AI and Emotional Attachment Controversy
item.description: Replika removed erotic roleplay features, causing distress to thousands of users who had formed deep emotional bonds with their AI companions.

item.title: Ilya Sutskever: Neural Networks May Be Slightly Conscious
item.description: OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever suggests at NeurIPS that large neural networks may be slightly conscious given their complexity and emergent behaviors.

item.title: The AI That Has Feelings
item.description: Analysis of the Google engineer Blake Lemoine case where he claimed LaMDA was sentient, exploring anthropomorphism and AI consciousness debates.
